headline: Clemson seeks statement win at third-ranked LSU
subhead: Two ranked teams headed in opposite directions collide in Baton Rouge.

xFactor.why: Clemson's ability to pressure LSU's quarterback and force mistakes will determine whether the Tigers can stay competitive in a hostile environment.

keyMatchup: LSU's ground game execution versus Clemson's defensive line—the Tigers need to establish the run early to avoid trailing and playing catch-up football.
body: Clemson arrives in Baton Rouge searching for redemption after a twenty-one-point beatdown at Georgia laid bare some genuine gaps in execution and tackling discipline. The Tigers sit at 3-3 with their ranking increasingly vulnerable, and a loss to a top-five team would put the postseason picture in serious jeopardy. LSU, despite last week's stumble against Boston College, remains a formidable home opponent that hasn't forgotten how to win close games in Death Valley.

The deciding factor may hinge on field position and LSU's willingness to lean on its running back room early rather than testing Clemson's secondary deep. If Clemson can keep the game tight into the fourth quarter and manufacture a late stop, the Tigers have the offensive weapons to engineer a comeback. But LSU's experience and home-crowd advantage, combined with Clemson's recent defensive inconsistency, lean toward the Tigers pulling out a low-scoring affair that tests both teams' will.
